metadata.website_type: cloud storage and file sharing service

tasks.name: Collect 5 'Invoice_2024' PDFs into a new 'Invoices 2024' folder and star it
tasks.description: Within the Documents area, create a folder named 'Invoices 2024', find PDF files whose names start with 'Invoice_2024', move exactly 5 of them into the new folder, and star that folder so it appears in favorites.
steps: From anywhere on the site, click the site logo to go to the main “My Files” or homepage view.
In the left sidebar, click the “Documents” folder to open it.
At the top of the Documents file list, click the “New folder” button.
In the folder name field, type “Invoices 2024” and click the “Create” button.
In the search bar above the file list, type “Invoice_2024” and press Enter.
In the filters panel, open the “File type” dropdown and select only the “PDF” option.
In the filtered results, select the first 5 files whose names start with “Invoice_2024”.
Click the “Move” button or folder icon in the toolbar, choose “Documents > Invoices 2024” as the destination, and confirm the move.
In the breadcrumb or sidebar, click “Documents” to return to the Documents folder view where “Invoices 2024” is listed.
In the Documents list, click the star icon next to the “Invoices 2024” folder to mark it as starred.

tasks.name: Share the 'Project Alpha' folder with two users with different permissions and an expiry date
tasks.description: Locate the 'Project Alpha' folder under Projects, share it with user 'alex_viewer' as a viewer and 'jamie_editor' as an editor, restrict access to invited people only, and set the link access to expire on December 31, 2025.
steps: Click the site logo to go to the main “My Files” view.
In the left sidebar, click the “Projects” folder to open it.
In the Projects list, locate the “Project Alpha” folder and open its context menu (three dots) to the right of the name.
From the context menu, click “Share”.
In the “Invite people” field, type “alex_viewer”, open the role dropdown next to their name, select “Viewer”, and click the “Add” or “Share” button to add them.
In the same invite field, type “jamie_editor”, open the role dropdown, select “Editor”, and click the “Add” or “Share” button to add them.
In the link access section of the sharing panel, set the access level dropdown to “Only invited people”.
In the “Access expires” or expiration section, choose “Custom date” and select “December 31, 2025” from the date picker.
Click the “Save”, “Done”, or close button on the sharing panel to apply the settings and return to the file list.

tasks.name: Archive the two largest video files over 500 MB and permanently delete the third largest
tasks.description: Use storage and file filters to find video files, identify the three largest files of at least 500 MB, move the two largest into a 'Videos/Archive' folder (creating it if needed), and permanently delete the third largest file.
steps: From the main navigation, click “Storage” or “Usage” in the sidebar to open the storage overview page.
On the storage overview page, click “View all files” or the button that opens the complete file list for your storage.
At the top of the file list, open the “Type” filter and select only video files (for example, .mp4).
Click the “Sort by” dropdown and choose “Size – Largest first” to sort videos from biggest to smallest.
From the sorted list, select the first 3 video files that have a size of at least 500 MB in the size column.

In the left sidebar, click the “Videos” folder to open it, then check if an “Archive” subfolder exists; if it does not, click “New folder”, type “Archive”, and click “Create” to make it inside “Videos”.
With the same 3 large video files still selected in the main list, hold Ctrl (or Cmd on Mac) and click the third-largest file to deselect it so that only the two largest remain selected.
Click the “Move” button in the toolbar, choose “Videos > Archive” as the destination in the folder picker, and confirm the move.
Return to the main video list, select the remaining third-largest video file that you previously deselected, and click the “Delete” or trash icon to move it to the trash.
In the left sidebar, click “Trash”, select that same video file in the Trash view, and click “Delete permanently” to remove it completely.

tasks.name: Create a restricted public link for 'Team Handbook.pdf' that is view-only, no download, expiring June 30, 2025
tasks.description: Find 'Team Handbook.pdf' in the root files, generate a public link that allows anyone with the link to view but not download the file, set the link to expire on June 30, 2025, and copy the link.
steps: Click the site logo to go to the main “My Files” view showing your root files and folders.
In the root file list, locate the file named “Team Handbook.pdf”.
Open the context menu (three dots) for “Team Handbook.pdf” and click “Share link” or “Get link”.
In the link-sharing panel that opens, set the main access dropdown to “Anyone with the link”.
Under permissions, open the permissions dropdown and select “Can view” to make the link view-only.
In the advanced or additional link settings, turn off or uncheck the option that allows viewers to download the file (for example, disable “Allow download”).
In the expiration or “Link expires” section, click “Set expiration date” and select “June 30, 2025” from the date picker.
Click the “Copy link” button to copy the generated URL to the clipboard, then click “Done” or close the sharing panel.

tasks.name: Gather the 3 most recently modified 'Q3' spreadsheets (last 60 days) into 'Reports/Q3 Reports'
tasks.description: Create a 'Q3 Reports' folder under Reports, search for .xlsx files with 'Q3' in the name that were modified in the last 60 days, sort them by most recently modified, and move the first 3 into the 'Q3 Reports' folder.
steps: From anywhere on the site, click the site logo to open the main “My Files” view.
In the left sidebar, click the “Reports” folder to open it.
At the top of the Reports file list, click the “New folder” button, type “Q3 Reports” as the name, and click “Create”.
In the global search bar at the top of the page, type “Q3” and press Enter to search across all files.
On the search results page, open the “File type” filter and select only “Spreadsheet (.xlsx)” files.
Open the “Last modified” or “Date modified” filter and set it to show only files modified within the last 60 days.
Click the “Sort by” dropdown and choose “Last modified – Newest first” to show the most recently modified files at the top.
From the sorted and filtered results, select the first 3 spreadsheet files whose names include “Q3”.
Click the “Move” button in the results toolbar, and in the destination picker choose “Reports > Q3 Reports”, then confirm the move.

tasks.name: Restore 'Roadmap.docx' to its version from January 15, 2024 at 10:00 AM
tasks.description: Open the 'Roadmap.docx' document, view its version history, locate the version saved at 'January 15, 2024, 10:00 AM', restore that version, and exit back to the file list.
steps: From the main navigation, click “My Files” to open the root file list.
In the search bar at the top of the page, type “Roadmap.docx” and press Enter.
In the search results, click on the “Roadmap.docx” file name to open it in the document viewer or editor.
Inside the document interface, open the “File” or “More” menu and click “Version history”.
In the version history panel that appears, scroll if necessary and click the version labeled with the timestamp “January 15, 2024, 10:00 AM”.
With that version selected, click the “Restore this version” button.
After the restore action completes, close the version history panel and then close the document viewer to return to the file list.

tasks.name: Make the 2 most recently opened PDFs and the 'Client Pitch' project folder available offline
tasks.description: From the Recent view, filter to PDFs, sort by last opened, mark the top 2 PDFs as available offline, then navigate to Projects and make the 'Client Pitch' folder available offline as well.
steps: In the left sidebar, click the “Recent” or “Recent files” option to open your recently accessed items.
At the top of the Recent view, open the “File type” filter and select only “PDF” files.
Click the “Sort by” dropdown and choose “Last opened – Newest first” so the most recently opened PDFs appear at the top.
From the sorted list, select the first 2 PDF files shown at the top.
In the toolbar above the list, click the “Available offline” toggle or icon to mark those 2 selected PDFs for offline access.
In the sidebar, click the “Projects” folder to open it, then click the “Client Pitch” subfolder to open that folder.
At the top of the “Client Pitch” folder view, open the folder options menu (three dots) and choose “Make available offline” for the entire folder.

tasks.name: Update download permissions for the newest shared file and label it 'Confidential'
tasks.description: In the 'Shared with me' section, identify the most recently shared single file (not a folder), open its share settings, disable downloading for its viewer collaborator, and add a 'Confidential' label to the file.
steps: In the left sidebar, click “Shared with me” to open the list of items that others have shared with you.
At the top-right of the Shared with me list, click the “Sort by” dropdown and choose “Shared date – Newest first”.
From the sorted list, find the first item that is a single file (not a folder), open its context menu (three dots), and click “Share”.
In the sharing panel, locate the collaborator who currently has “Viewer” access and click the permission dropdown next to their name.
In that permission dropdown, select the option that disables downloading for that viewer (for example, uncheck “Allow download” or choose “View only, no download”).
In the same sharing or details panel, click “More settings” or “Details” to open the file details sidebar if it is not already visible.
In the labels or tags section of the details panel, click “Add label”, type “Confidential”, and press Enter to create and apply that label to the file.
Close the details or sharing panel to return to the “Shared with me” list.

entities.name: StorageItem
entities.description: Unified entity for both files and folders in the cloud storage hierarchy.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the file or folder.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the file or folder (e.g., 'Project Alpha', 'Team Handbook.pdf').

fields.name: itemType
fields.description: Distinguishes files from folders.

fields.name: extension
fields.description: File extension without dot (e.g., 'pdf', 'docx', 'xlsx'); empty or null for folders.

fields.name: fileType
fields.description: High-level file category used for type filters (e.g., PDF, spreadsheet, video). Not set for folders.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: MIME type for files (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'video/mp4').

fields.name: sizeBytes
fields.description: File size in bytes. May be 0 or null for folders.

fields.name: parentId
fields.description: ID of the parent folder (another StorageItem with itemType='folder'); null for root-level items.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Computed or stored full path (e.g., '/Documents/Invoices 2024/Invoice_2024_001.pdf').

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the item was created.

fields.name: modifiedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the item was last modified (used for 'Last modified – Newest first' sorting and 60‑day filters).

fields.name: lastOpenedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the item was last opened (used in Recent view and 'Last opened – Newest first' sorting).

fields.name: starred
fields.description: Whether the item is starred (e.g., 'Invoices 2024' folder starred for favorites).

fields.name: isAvailableOffline
fields.description: Whether the file or folder is marked as available offline.

fields.name: isDeleted
fields.description: Soft-delete flag indicating if the item is in the Trash.

fields.name: deletedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the item was moved to Trash (used for Trash sorting and display).

fields.name: originalParentId
fields.description: Parent folder ID before deletion; used to restore items from Trash to their original location.

fields.name: hasVersionHistory
fields.description: Indicates whether the file has version history available (true for versioned files like 'Roadmap.docx').

fields.name: currentVersionId
fields.description: ID of the current FileVersion record for this file; null for folders or files without versioning.

entities.name: FileVersion
entities.description: Version history entries for versioned files (e.g., 'Roadmap.docx' snapshots).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for this file version.

fields.name: storageItemId
fields.description: ID of the StorageItem (must be a file) this version belongs to.

fields.name: versionNumber
fields.description: Monotonic version number (1, 2, 3, ...).

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Timestamp when this version was saved (used to find 'January 15, 2024, 10:00 AM').

fields.name: sizeBytes
fields.description: Size of this specific version in bytes.

fields.name: contentHash
fields.description: Optional hash of content for deduplication or comparison.

fields.name: note
fields.description: Optional user-visible version note or label.

fields.name: isCurrent
fields.description: Whether this version is the currently active version of the file.

entities.name: ShareLink
entities.description: Link-based sharing configuration for files and folders (public or restricted links with expiry and permissions).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for the share link.

fields.name: storageItemId
fields.description: ID of the StorageItem (file or folder) this share link applies to.

fields.name: accessLevel
fields.description: Who can access the item via this link (e.g., 'only_invited_people' for Project Alpha, 'anyone_with_link' for public Team Handbook link).

fields.name: permission
fields.description: Base permission level granted by the link (view or edit).

fields.name: allowDownload
fields.description: Whether viewers can download the content via this link (disabled for view-only, no-download public links).

fields.name: expiresAt
fields.description: Optional expiration timestamp for the link (e.g., December 31, 2025 or June 30, 2025).

fields.name: url
fields.description: The generated shareable URL for this link.

fields.name: isEnabled
fields.description: Whether the link is currently active. Disabling would effectively revoke link access.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: When this share link was created.

entities.name: CollaboratorPermission
entities.description: Per-collaborator access configuration for a shared file or folder (role and download permissions).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for this collaborator permission entry.

fields.name: storageItemId
fields.description: ID of the StorageItem this collaborator has access to (e.g., 'Project Alpha' folder).

fields.name: collaboratorName
fields.description: Identifier/username for the collaborator (e.g., 'alex_viewer', 'jamie_editor').

fields.name: role
fields.description: Collaborator's role for this item.

fields.name: allowDownload
fields.description: Whether this collaborator is allowed to download the item (can be disabled for viewer in Task 8).

fields.name: addedAt
fields.description: When this collaborator was first granted access.

fields.name: lastUpdatedAt
fields.description: When this collaborator's permissions were last modified.

entities.name: IncomingShare
entities.description: Represents items that have been shared with the current user and appear in the 'Shared with me' view.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for this incoming share record.

fields.name: storageItemId
fields.description: ID of the shared StorageItem (file or folder).

fields.name: ownerName
fields.description: Name/identifier of the owner who shared the item.

fields.name: sharedDate
fields.description: When the item was shared with the current user (used for 'Shared date – Newest first' sorting).

fields.name: permission
fields.description: The current user's role on the shared item.

fields.name: allowDownload
fields.description: Whether the current user is allowed to download the shared item.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Optional note or message sent with the share.

entities.name: Label
entities.description: User-defined labels/tags that can be applied to files and folders (e.g., 'Confidential').

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for the label.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Label name as shown in the UI (e.g., 'Confidential').

fields.name: color
fields.description: Optional color identifier or hex code for the label.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description or usage hint for the label.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: When this label was created.

entities.name: ItemLabel
entities.description: Join entity linking labels to specific files or folders.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID for this label assignment.

fields.name: storageItemId
fields.description: ID of the StorageItem (file or folder) that the label is applied to.

fields.name: labelId
fields.description: ID of the Label applied to the item.

fields.name: appliedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the label was applied to the item.

relationships.description: Each item (file or folder) may belong to a parent folder StorageItem; root items have null parentId.
Each file version is associated with a single StorageItem (must be a file).
Each share link is configured for a specific StorageItem (file or folder).
Each collaborator permission entry grants a collaborator access to a single StorageItem.
Each incoming share record refers to the shared StorageItem that appears in 'Shared with me'.
Each ItemLabel links a label assignment to a specific StorageItem.
Each ItemLabel refers to a specific Label applied to an item.
